title: NASA’s inspector general warns launch sites nearing capacity
summary: NASA's inspector general warns that increasing launch activity is straining the capacity of spaceports. The report highlights growing concerns over the sustainability of current launch site infrastructure.

title: Boeing wins $2 billion Space Force contract for communications satellites
summary: Boeing secured a $2 billion contract to build two next-generation military communications satellites for the U.S. Space Force, defeating Lockheed Martin in a competitive bid.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes New Associate Member
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of Sophia Space as a new associate member. Sophia Space is developing orbital computing infrastructure for advanced space-based data processing.

title: Ubotica raises $11 million to scale maritime-intelligence platform
summary: Ubotica Technologies, an Irish AI company for spacecraft, raised $11 million to expand its maritime-intelligence platform. The funding will support commercial sales of their Live Maritime platform.

title: Sophia selects Apex bus for on-orbit computing demonstration
summary: Sophia Space selected an Apex bus for its first orbital computing node demonstration. The mission aims to showcase Thermal Integrated LEO capabilities.

title: Loft Orbital to test AI models on spacecraft for Earth observation
summary: Loft Orbital is collaborating with NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory to test AI models on spacecraft for enhanced Earth observation. The initiative aims to improve Earth science monitoring capabilities.

title: Satellogic partners with SynMax to build intelligence services around upcoming Merlin constellation
summary: Satellogic has partnered with SynMax to develop intelligence services leveraging its upcoming Merlin constellation. The collaboration aims to meet the demand from defense and intelligence agencies for persistent surveillance capabilities.

title: China appears to be developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets
summary: China is reportedly developing 7-meter-diameter reusable rockets, as indicated by state-funded tender documents, stainless steel forging deliveries, and launch pad planning. This development suggests a significant advancement in China's reusable launch vehicle technology.

title: Report: U.S. needs framework for responding to hostile acts in space
summary: A report highlights the need for a U.S. framework to address hostile acts in space amid rising concerns over China's counterspace capabilities. Experts at a Mitchell Institute workshop struggled to define the boundary between competition and conflict in space.

title: NASA and Boeing still uncertain about when Starliner will return to flight
summary: NASA and Boeing are uncertain about the return-to-flight timeline for the CST-100 Starliner, with a potential delay of up to a year. Safety advisers note progress in addressing issues but no definitive schedule.

title: What is Starfall? A look at SpaceX's mysterious new return capsule
summary: SpaceX has launched its new Starfall return capsule for the first time. The purpose of the vehicle remains unclear as the company has not disclosed its intended use.

title: Space.com just launched an app! Get your inside scoop on space exploration
summary: Space.com has launched a new app providing daily updates on space exploration, including mission news, discoveries, and night sky guides. The app aims to offer an immersive experience for space enthusiasts.

title: These anti-Prime deals are giving Amazon a good run for their money
summary: Anti-Prime Day deals are offering discounts on skywatching gear from retailers like Walmart, Adorama, and BH Photo & Video, challenging Amazon's dominance in the market.

title: Rocket Lab launches US Space Force mission with less than 17 hours' notice — a new record
summary: Rocket Lab launched the second satellite for the U.S. Space Force's Victus Haze mission with less than 17 hours' notice, setting a new launch readiness record. The mission's two spacecraft are now in a chase to rendezvous and perform dynamic engagements.

title: This might be your last chance to pick up a retired Lego Star Wars set before it flies off forever, and it's under $100 on Amazon for Prime Day
summary: A retired Lego Star Wars set, the Coruscant Guard Gunship, is available for under $100 on Amazon during Prime Day, marking its lowest price ever. This could be the last opportunity to purchase the discontinued set.

title: A 'new' star could finally appear in the night sky this week, thanks to a once-in-a-lifetime explosion
summary: A rare astronomical event may cause the 'Blaze Star' T. Coronae Borealis to brighten dramatically this week, potentially becoming visible to the naked eye. This once-in-a-lifetime explosion could make it shine as brightly as the North Star, Polaris.

title: China's space plane appears to have released a mystery object in orbit
summary: China's Shenlong space plane has reportedly released an unidentified object while in orbit, as observed by a private space surveillance firm. The purpose or nature of the object remains unclear.

title: What telescope could cause our astrophotography expert to break their hard-and-fast rule about not traveling with a telescope? This one! Now even cheaper in this Prime Day telescope deal
summary: The ZWO Seestar S30 Pro telescope is highlighted in a Prime Day deal, praised for its stunning views that convinced an astrophotography expert to break their rule against traveling with a telescope.

title: NASA wants to dump the ISS in the sea. Experts say the plan 'raises serious concerns for ocean health'
summary: NASA plans to deorbit the International Space Station in the coming years, which has raised concerns from ocean conservation experts about potential impacts on ocean health. The move has sparked reactions from environmental advocates.

title: The Unistellar Odyssey Pro helped me feel in touch with the universe and it's now 15% off
summary: The Unistellar Odyssey Pro telescope offers advanced tracking and autofocus for deep-sky imaging and is now 15% off. This device enhances the user's experience in observing the universe.

title: NASA Names Sean Gallagher as Chief Information Officer
summary: NASA has appointed Sean Gallagher as its Chief Information Officer (CIO), effective immediately. He will oversee the agency's entire IT portfolio, having served in an acting role since January.

title: CSDA Selects Eight Commercial Satellite Data Providers for On-Ramp 2 Contract Awards
summary: NASA's Commercial Smallsat Data Acquisition (CSDA) program selected eight commercial satellite data providers for On-Ramp 2 contracts. These providers will support NASA's Earth science priorities by supplying diverse data.

title: 58th Girl Scouts Unite Event
summary: NASA will participate in the 58th Girl Scouts Unite Event with Hyperwall storytelling sessions led by experts like Barbara Hilton and Lindsay Hays. The event is scheduled for July 23-25, 2026, at the Exhibit Hall (Booth #206).

title: 2026 ALA Hyperwall Schedule
summary: NASA will participate in the 2026 ALA Annual Conference with Hyperwall storytelling sessions at booth #2243. The event takes place from June 25-29, 2026.

title: This is How NASA Flight Tests New Technology
summary: NASA emphasizes the importance of flight tests in advancing space technology, highlighting their role in transforming innovative ideas into safer and more efficient solutions for public benefit.

title: Hanging in the Balance
summary: The Artemis II crew captured a striking image of the Moon on flight day 6, highlighting its half-illuminated surface and rugged terrain. The photo emphasizes the stark contrast between light and dark regions, offering a unique perspective of the lunar landscape.

title: ARMD Research Solicitations (Updated June 23)
summary: NASA's Aeronautics Research Mission Directorate (ARMD) updated its solicitations page on June 23, 2026, inviting collaboration and research contributions to improve air transportation systems. Opportunities are officially announced via the NASA Solicitation and Proposal Integrated System.

title: I Am Artemis: Jason Peterson
summary: Jason Peterson played a key role in NASA's Artemis II mission as the range operations manager at Stennis Space Center, leveraging his military experience to handle long workdays. His efforts contributed to the successful test flight around the Moon.

title: Hubble Details Early Galaxy Transforming Neighborhood
summary: Astronomers using NASA's Hubble Space Telescope detected ultraviolet light from a galaxy that existed 1.4 billion years after the Big Bang, revealing unexpected early galaxy evolution.

title: NASA’s Webb Pinpoints Millions of Stars Within Cigar Galaxy
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope has revealed previously unseen details of the Cigar Galaxy (Messier 82), located 12 million light-years away. The galaxy is undergoing rapid star formation, likely due to a merger event.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: The upcoming week features six orbital launches globally, including the first flight of Pegasus XL. The mission is scheduled alongside launches by Falcon 9 and Chinese rockets.

title: SpaceX tests upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle with demonstration mission on Tuesday
summary: SpaceX conducted a demonstration mission for its upcoming Starfall reentry vehicle using a Falcon 9 rocket. The test launch took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Canadarm2 repairs planned as CRS-34 departs ISS
summary: The Expedition 74 crew on the ISS is planning repairs for the Canadarm2 as the CRS-34 mission departs. The crew has been active with multiple operations during a busy month.

title: Terran R second stage heads to Stennis as Relativity advances toward 2026 debut
summary: Relativity Space has transported the second stage of its Terran R heavy-lift rocket to NASA's Stennis Space Center, marking progress toward its planned 2026 debut.

title: Launch Preview: 10 launches scheduled from sites across the world
summary: Ten launches are scheduled globally in the upcoming week, marking a busy period for the space industry. The launches will take place from various sites around the world.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket after weeks of delays
summary: Isar Aerospace canceled the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket after multiple delays. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: Booster 20 Completes Cryo, As Ship 40 Nears Static Fire
summary: Booster 20 has completed cryogenic testing, while Ship 40 is nearing a static fire test. The focus follows the recent completion of Flight 12.

title: China successfully debuts tallest rocket, LandSpace prepares for second landing attempt
summary: China successfully launched its tallest rocket, while LandSpace is preparing for a second attempt at a rocket landing. The missions are part of a busy month for China's internet constellation.

title: Japan’s H3 to returns to flight with debut launch of lightest configuration
summary: Japan's H3 rocket successfully returned to flight with its debut launch of the lightest configuration. The mission marked a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Stoke Space completes Nova Stage 1 structural verification
summary: Stoke Space has completed the proto-qualification structural verification of the first stage of its Nova rocket. This milestone marks progress in the development of the company's reusable launch vehicle.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but the specific details were not provided in the input.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities and industrial opportunities. These events aim to facilitate direct exchange between ESA and the industrial community.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm technology that aims to revolutionize space operations. This innovation is expected to enhance the capabilities of spacecraft and satellite servicing.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational and prepared to resume formation flying, enabling the creation of artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme enables European research institutes to participate in space science missions. The recent 4DSpace-Daedalus mission launch in Norway is a successful example of this support.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: ESA's Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section tests materials for spacecraft to ensure they survive space conditions. The section is supported by young researchers who contribute innovative ideas.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involved testing parachutes in extreme conditions, likely for a Mars or high-altitude mission. The 'bake-out' process ensures parachute materials can withstand high temperatures during re-entry or descent.

title: NASA Watchdog: Launch Infrastructure Nearing Capacity
summary: NASA's launch facilities are expected to reach capacity by the end of the decade due to a surge in commercial launches. A report from the agency's inspector general highlights this growing challenge.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Taps Apex for 2027 Demo Flight
summary: Sophia Space secured $7 million in funding and partnered with Apex for a 2027 demo flight to deploy edge compute hardware in orbit.

title: Ubotica Raises $11M for Satellite-Based AI Tech
summary: Ubotica, a Dublin-based space AI company, raised $11 million to advance its AI-powered intelligence platform for maritime security. The funding aims to accelerate the commercialization of satellite-based AI technology.

title: A Faster Path to Orbit: How Hosted Payloads Are Changing Access to Space
summary: Hosted payloads are emerging as a faster and more economical alternative to traditional space missions, reducing the time, investment, and technical expertise required. Companies are increasingly adopting this model to deploy new capabilities in space without building everything from scratch.

title: Exclusive: Northwood Unveils New Antenna, Expanded Network Capacity
summary: Northwood has unveiled a new antenna to expand its network capacity, supporting more data downlinks over a wider range of frequencies. This development is part of the company's rapid global expansion of its ground station footprint.

title: ElevationSpace Closes $40M Series B
summary: ElevationSpace, a Japanese satellite reentry startup, secured a $40M Series B funding round, bringing its total raised to $63.5M.

title: Boeing Completes Quantum Lab Test Ahead of 2027 Flight
summary: Boeing successfully demonstrated quantum entanglement swapping in ground tests for its Q4S quantum networking satellite. The company plans to launch the satellite in 2027.

title: True Anomaly Declares Mission X-3 Success
summary: True Anomaly announced the successful completion of Mission X-3, where its autonomous orbital vehicle Jackal achieved all test objectives and is now fully commissioned.

title: Instinct Space Unveils Plans for Low-Cost Lunar Landers
summary: Instinct Space is shifting its focus from navigation support for lunar missions to developing low-cost lunar landers. The company aims to directly contribute to lunar surface missions.

title: Katalyst Raises $12M to Extend Satellite Servicing to GEO
summary: Katalyst Space Technologies raised $12 million to expand its satellite servicing capabilities to geostationary orbit (GEO). The company plans to launch a mission closer to Earth this month, followed by its first GEO mission in 2027.

title: Northwood Space Debuts Parabolic Antenna, Designed for High-Volume Data
summary: Northwood Space introduced the Prism parabolic antenna, a 2.4-meter ground system designed to enable high-volume data connectivity. The new technology aims to enhance communication capabilities for space applications.

title: Space Sector Faces 400% Surge in Cyberattacks Amid Iran War, Experts Say
summary: The space sector has experienced a 400% increase in cyberattacks following U.S. and Israel-led military operations in Iran, according to cybersecurity experts. These attacks have become more frequent and sophisticated.

title: Sophia Space Raises $7M, Selects Apex Space Bus for Orbital Compute Demo
summary: Sophia Space raised $7 million and selected Apex Space's Nova satellite bus for an orbital computing demonstration mission planned for 2027.

title: SpaceX Launches New Microgravity Lab Demo, Starfall
summary: SpaceX successfully demonstrated its new Starfall reentry vehicle, designed to provide regular access to microgravity for scientific research and in-space manufacturing. This marks a significant step in advancing commercial space capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Launches Responsive Victus Haze Mission for Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched the Tactically Responsive Space (TacRS) Victus Haze mission for the U.S. Space Force from New Zealand. This responsive space mission aims to demonstrate rapid deployment capabilities for national security purposes.

title: Building a Global Framework for D2D With Skylo’s Mindel De La Torre
summary: Skylo's Mindel De La Torre discusses the rapid deployment of direct-to-device (D2D) satellite connectivity in millions of phones, transitioning from a milestone tech demonstration to a widely adopted feature.

title: EchoStar Swaps SATS Ticker for ECHO to Emphasize Business Shift
summary: EchoStar is changing its stock ticker from 'SATS' to 'ECHO' to reflect its shift into a global connectivity business, effective June 24.

title: Jio Evaluates a Sovereign LEO Constellation for India
summary: Jio Platforms, India's largest telecom operator, is considering launching a sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) constellation to provide satellite broadband. The company is exploring a 'dual approach' to expand its satellite services.

title: MDA Space to Acquire Blue Canyon from Raytheon to Reach US Defense Market
summary: MDA Space is acquiring spacecraft manufacturer Blue Canyon Technologies from Raytheon to expand into the U.S. defense market. The acquisition will provide MDA Space with a stronger foothold in the American defense sector.

title: Former SES Exec John-Paul Hemingway to Lead Vast’s Satellite Business
summary: Former SES Chief Commercial Officer John-Paul Hemingway has joined Vast as Senior Vice President of Satellite Business Development to lead Vast's new satellite manufacturing business. Hemingway will oversee business development and commercialization efforts for the company.
